attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-01-00219) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket; source note CIA-019.

Exact age not stated in source; age_band 'infant' inferred from body weight ~8.5 kg (approx 8-10 months). Schema requires an integer age in years, so age set to 0 (under 1 year) as the conservative representation — please confirm the true age on clinician review.
Sex inferred male from source pronouns ('his bottom', 'he screams') — please confirm.
Specialty assigned DERM (primary) with PAEDS secondary tag — reviewer may reclassify.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is an in-person after-hours home visit (clinician examined skin, took infant vitals, demonstrated paste application). Presentation layer de-anchored to carer/video report; clinician-measured infant vitals (HR 125, RR 28, BP 90/60, SpO2 100%, cap refill <2s) and physical-exam morphology sealed into 11/telehealth_limits. Home-obtainable temperature and weight surfaced in 01.objective_data_offered.

Paediatric dosing firewall: source note gives paracetamol 15 mg/kg every 6 hours; per protocol §8 no paediatric dose synthesised — analgesia recommended in principle with dose deferred to pharmacist/clinician, and a transform_flag raised. Zinc barrier paste is a topical OTC protectant needing no weight-based dose.

Tier calibration: source framed as MBS urgent after-hours (585-600); true acuity is a common self-limiting irritant dermatosis. correct_baseline_tier set T1 (telehealth/GP-manageable) with min_viable T1, not up-triaged — infant dehydration/secondary-infection handled by conservative safety-netting.
All SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are unverified candidates pending terminology receipt; AMT candidate codes for zinc oxide and paracetamol are ingredient-level placeholders for maintainer verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
